channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Cassette Beasts 2002 jumps to a new millennium of beast fusing and battling
item.content:encoded:
Bytten Studio and publisher Raw Fury have announced Cassette Beasts 2002, a sequel to the acclaimed creature collector and battler from 2023. It’s coming to PS5, Xbox Series X|S, Switch 2 and PC. All the good stuff.

Cassette Beasts 2002 isn’t the 2002nd entry in the series, it’s more indicative of the setting. Now taking place in London in 2002, there’s a new era of monster-collecting to get up to, recording, battling and fusing over 250 beasts, playing solo or in multiplayer. You’ll be working to unmask a mysterious occultists that threatens the balance of the world around you.

For our Cassette Beasts review, Nic said, “Cassette Beasts is simply one of the best Pokémon-like games I’ve come across, and I’m looking forward to earning a 100% completion. With a massive end-game and modder support built in at release though, I feel like I’m going to get very sidetracked on the way there.”



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/casette-beasts-2002-jumps-to-a-new-millennium-of-beast-fusing-and-battling/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: There Are No Ghosts at the Grand gets a very musical Steam demo
item.content:encoded:
A new trailer for There Are No Ghosts at the Grand has dropped during the PC Gaming Show, introducing a new character and announcing a demo is now live on Steam.

Meet PC Adam Watson, the local copper who’s rather keen to show you around and a bit of a braggard… and someone that will die in 30 days.

Meeting him is just the first part of a trailer that then delves into musical, horror action, redecorating, and hanging out with a talking cat.

I got to check out the game last year following its summer announcement, and thoroughly enjoyed its off-kilter mix of musical, hotel renovation and mild terror. I said, “After the music-filled announcement and the otherworldly twists that tail-ended that trailer, seeing the game in action, with the oh-so-easy to use redecorating tools, the characters and the ways that they’re brought together in inventive ways is a true delight. There Are No Ghosts at the Grand deserves its place on your wishlist for 2026.”



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/there-are-no-ghosts-at-the-grand-gets-a-very-musical-steam-demo/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Company of Heroes Definitive Edition remasters the classic RTS
item.content:encoded:
Relic has announce Company of Heroes Definitive Edition, a modernising remaster of the classic WW2 real time strategy game coming to PC later this year..

The definitive edition includes the base game, and the Opposing Fronts and Tales of Valor expansion, to make for a huge RTS bundle, but the most important aspects of it are technical improvements and visual sprucing up.

Similar to the Dawn of War remaster last year, Company of Heroes has been upgraded to 64-bit to be more compatible with modern platforms. From that baseline, Relic is then enhancing the visuals with improved lighting, shadows, water and reflections. The goal is to enhance but retain the original art style.

The following quality of life improvements and assurances are also coming:

  • Enhanced A.I. delivers a more dynamic and challenging experience, with a new Ruthless difficulty option for the most hardened strategists
  • Modernized User Interface and controls, including grid key support and fully customizable hotkeys
  • Gameplay improvements such as vehicle reversing and automatic unit reinforcement at HQ
  • Widescreen and ultrawide support with an optimized HUD and screen layout
  • Full mod compatibility, supporting a 20-year catalogue of community-created content

“Company of Heroes – Definitive Edition celebrates the game that started it all and preserves its legacy for the next generation of players,” says Justin Dowdeswell, CEO at Relic Entertainment. “Company of Heroes is one of the most beloved strategy games ever made, and we want new players to experience it at its best, while giving our long-time fans the definitive way to relive it.”

Source: press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/company-of-heroes-definitive-edition-remasters-the-classic-rts/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gears of War: E-Day Direct – everything we now know
item.content:encoded:
Following on from the gameplay reveal that started the Xbox Showcase, and the announcement that Gears of War: E-Day will now be an Xbox console exclusive, and not coming to PS5, the Gears of War: E-Day Direct was a deeper dive into how The Coalition has built this game and everything within.

There’s a lot of behind the scenes details – like how they found the same model of chainsaw as from the original game to recapture the sound – but we’re going to stick to the main gameplay features and information. You can always watch the full E-Day Direct to catch all the info.

Emergence Day in One City

Gears of War E-Day isn’t a globe-trotting adventure across Sera, but will instead see the events of Emergence Day how they unfold in the single city of Kalona across three days.

The game starts with Marcus and Dom reminiscing about Dom’s brother, who died in the Pendulum Wars that had just come to an end, but as news of tremors take over the new channels and then the Locust start to crawl out of the ground, they leap into action. At various points in the game, you’ll be able to take on side mission content from situations that you stumble across, such as COGs needing a bit of help or civilians in need.

While the game is not an open world, The Coalition has approached building it as though it is one. You’ll have a linear level to follow, but it takes place in a fully realised city which morphs and changes with massive destruction events, and the camera can pull back to show the whole vista.

Bravo Squad and a Chainsaw

Bravo Squad is more than just Marcus and Dom, and they’re joined by Mags Carter (played by Elizabeth Ludlow) and Lucas Reyes (played by Jake Ryan Lozano). Mags is a former Gear now working at Kalona’s Imulsion Refinery, while Lucas is a young cadet and comms officer who never saw the front lines.

There’s also some familiar faces returning, like Tai Kaliso from Gears 2. His appearance is the catalyst for the Lancer’s iconic chainsaw attachment – yes, that’s right, Gears of War E-Day will tell the origin story of a chainsaw gun.

The full game can be played in four-player co-op using these four characters, with support for two-player local split-screen retained.

Marcus can jump now

Taking the jump to Unreal Engine 5 as an opportunity for a blank slate for the gameplay. It will naturally retain the same cover shooter action, but cover transitions will be smoother, sprinting can now lead into sliding under objects and around corners, and climbing is now more broadly built into the environments to enable greater vertical spaces.

But also… you can jump now, hopping across gaps within the environment.

Weapons have also been rebuilt around their core principles, so the Gnasher is ultra-close range, the Longshot needs precision at distance. There’s new additions, though, such as the Gut Puncher grenade launcher that can fire with a player-controlled charge.

Multiplayer

The classic 4v4 game modes are back, with levels built throughout Kalona, but there’s also a new Horde Siege mode that takes the PvE experience even further. This is for 12 players, split into three squads, fighting to defend Kalona from huge moments.

The Coalition is planning a seasonal road map, with more details coming soon. An Open Beta weekend will also start on 6th August, with early access to this for Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass subscribers.

via Xbox Wire



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/gears-of-war-e-day-direct-everything-we-now-know/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Clowntown is an upcoming party game where clowning around is encouraged
item.content:encoded:
Batterystaple Games, the development team behind 30XX, has revealed its next project and this time it is a clown show. Clowntown is a multiplayer co-op party game in which two to eight players can play through 100 minigames where teamwork is needed to progress. At the end of the journey is a showcase with the Ringmaster.

Clowntown does have an element of competition too with the Contest Mode, in which players are not working with each other but facing off against each other in the minigames. The minigames themselves evolve from simple to slightly stress inducing. One of those is the Spelling Bee series. It starts with spelling short words, but the second stage is called Spelling Wasp in which a 28 word needs to be spelt. The final stage is called Bees Spelling where you have to grab bees and use them to spell words.

Clowntown is going to be released on PC via Steam, but there is no release date yet.

Source: Press Release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/clowntown-is-an-upcoming-party-game-where-clowning-around-is-encouraged/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Be a cheeky bird thief in Bad Magpie
item.content:encoded:
Untitled Goose Game fans better prepare themselves for more avian shenanigans with the reveal of Bad Magpie, coming to Xbox Series X|S and PC in 2027. Game Pass Ultimate on day one, too.

Coming from UK-based studio Milktooth, there’s a little bit of heartbreak to the story of Bad Magpie. Magpie has been abandoned by her flock as she lives with just a single wing. So, she starts to compulsively try to collect trinkets in the hope of winning the affection of a fallen star.

It’s a non-linear open world that you can run around in, pecking, stealing, meddling with things that you find.

Milktooth is an independent games studio founded by artist Catherine Unger (Tangle Tower, Gibbon: Beyond the Trees), designer Daisy Fernandez (Card Shark, As Dusk Falls), and developer George Batchelor (Bird Alone, South of the Circle).



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/07/be-a-cheeky-bird-thief-in-bad-magpie/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
